description: AWI-ESM3-veg is the third generation of the AWI Earth System Model, configured here in its high resolution variant with dynamic vegetation. It is a successor to the AWI-CM3 system described in Streffing et al. (2022, doi:10.5194/gmd-15-6399-2022), now with updated atmosphere and ocean cycles plus the addition of LPJ-GUESS for dynamic vegetation. The components are coupled through OASIS3-MCT5.  1. Dynamic components Atmosphere: OpenIFS 48r1 on a TCO319 reduced Gaussian grid with 137 hybrid sigma-pressure levels. Ocean: FESOM2.7 on the DARS2 unstructured triangular mesh of about 3.15 M surface vertices and 6.23 M elements, on 57 z-levels. Horizontal resolution varies from about 5 km in dynamically active regions to about 25 km in the bulk interior. Sea ice: FESIM2.7 with zero-layer thermodynamics, embedded in FESOM2 and sharing its surface mesh. Land surface and dynamic vegetation: LPJ-GUESS 4.1, running on the OpenIFS horizontal grid.  2. Prescribed components Tropospheric anthropogenic aerosol: MACv2-SP (Stevens et al. 2017), the simple-plumes climatology that supplies direct and first indirect aerosol effects from a prescribed plume description. Stratospheric aerosol: prescribed from the CMIP7 stratospheric aerosol forcing dataset. Atmospheric chemistry: not interactive; ozone is taken from the CMIP7 ozone forcing dataset. Greenhouse gases: prescribed concentrations throughout.  3. Omitted components Ocean biogeochemistry: REcoM is planned to couple into FESOM2 in a future submission within the awi-esm3 family, but is not part of this fast-track configuration. Dynamic land ice: PISM is planned to couple into AWI-ESM3 in a future submission, but is not part of this fast-track configuration.
